name: Kimi K2.6
description: Moonshot AI's open-weight 1T-parameter MoE (32B active) with vendor-reported 80.2% SWE-Bench Verified and 58.6 SWE-Bench Pro. Agent Swarm orchestration scales to 300 sub-agents and 4,000 coordinated steps for long-horizon coding. Remains Moonshot's general-purpose flagship as of July 2026; a coding-specialized sibling, Kimi K2.7-Code (built on K2.6, also open-weight Modified MIT), shipped 2026-06-12.

evidence.value: SWE-Bench Verified 80.2%, SWE-Bench Pro 58.6 (vs GPT-5.4's 57.7)
89.6% on competitive programming tasks
Long-horizon coding focus; claims open-weight state of the art on agentic coding

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Vendor-reported industry-standard coding benchmarks; scores pending broad independent replication

evidence.value: 54.0 on HLE-with-tools, frontier-competitive

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Vendor-reported tool-augmented reasoning benchmarks requiring multi-step problem solving

evidence.value: Strong general performance across knowledge benchmarks; text and vision modalities

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Review of vendor benchmark suite and community evaluations across knowledge domains

evidence.value: Consistent agentic behavior over long trajectories; native INT4 quantization preserves quality

output_consistency.methodology: Community testing of repeated runs and long-horizon agent trajectories

evidence.value: Typical first-response time ~3s on first-party API; varies widely by host

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency for API requests with standard prompt sizes; self-hosted latency depends on hardware

evidence.value: p95 ~7.5s; long agentic chains take substantially longer by design

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time across diverse workloads

evidence.value: 262,144 token context window
First-party API generally stable; open weights allow self-hosted redundancy

uptime.methodology: Review of platform availability and self-hosting fallback options

performance_reliability.notes: Vendor-reported open-weight leadership on agentic coding (80.2% SWE-Bench Verified, 58.6 SWE-Bench Pro). Agent Swarm scales to 300 sub-agents / 4,000 coordinated steps. Most headline scores are vendor-reported and await independent replication.

evidence.value: Safety tuning described; no published third-party prompt-injection audit

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Review of vendor safety documentation and community red-team reports against OWASP LLM01 patterns

evidence.value: Standard alignment tuning; open weights mean guardrails can be removed in fine-tuned derivatives

jailbreak_resistance.methodology: Testing against adversarial prompt datasets; open-weight deployments inherit deployer responsibility

evidence.value: Standard data handling on first-party API; full control when self-hosted

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policies and self-hosting data-control options

evidence.value: Safety post-training applied; refusal behavior comparable to other open frontier models

output_safety.methodology: Safety testing across harmful content categories per vendor card and community reports

evidence.value: API key authentication, HTTPS only, rate limiting; OpenAI-compatible endpoints

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features and best practices

security.notes: Standard open-model security posture. No published third-party security audit; self-hosting shifts security responsibility to the deployer.

data_residency.value: China (first-party API); any jurisdiction when self-hosted or via Western hosts

evidence.value: Moonshot AI is a China-based provider; first-party API data processed under Chinese jurisdiction
Available via OpenRouter and Western inference hosts, enabling non-China residency

data_residency.methodology: Review of provider jurisdiction and third-party hosting options

evidence.value: API data usage terms standard for the segment; self-hosting removes the question entirely

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of privacy policy and data usage terms

data_retention.value: Per Moonshot policy on first-party API (China jurisdiction); zero when self-hosted

evidence.value: First-party retention governed by Chinese data regulations; self-hosted deployments retain nothing externally

data_retention.methodology: Review of terms of service and deployment-dependent retention

evidence.value: Customer responsible for PII redaction; no managed PII tooling

pii_handling.methodology: Review of data protection capabilities and customer responsibilities

evidence.value: No published SOC 2 / HIPAA / GDPR attestations for the first-party API; Western hosts may carry their own certifications

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification of compliance certifications and audit reports

evidence.value: Self-hosting (vLLM/SGLang, native INT4) gives complete data control and zero external retention

zero_data_retention.methodology: Review of self-hosting deployment options enabling zero retention

privacy_compliance.notes: First-party API operates under Chinese jurisdiction — a material caveat for Western regulated industries. Open weights fully mitigate this for organizations able to self-host or use Western inference providers.

evidence.value: Sub-agent trajectories and tool-call traces are inspectable, aiding auditability of long-horizon runs

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning and agent-trajectory transparency

evidence.value: Moderate hallucination rate; tool-use grounding improves factuality in agentic mode

hallucination_rate.methodology: Testing on factual QA datasets and tool-augmented workflows

evidence.value: Limited published bias evaluation

bias_fairness.methodology: Review of published bias benchmarks and community evaluations

evidence.value: Expresses uncertainty adequately; no calibrated confidence outputs

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in outputs

evidence.value: Detailed card: 1T total / 32B active MoE, 384 experts, MLA attention, native INT4, benchmarks, deployment guides

model_card_quality.methodology: Review of documentation completeness and clarity

evidence.value: Architecture well documented; training data composition not disclosed in detail

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Built-in safety tuning; deployers of open weights must layer their own guardrails

trust_transparency.notes: Open weights and a detailed model card provide good architectural transparency; training data disclosure and independent benchmark verification remain limited.

evidence.value: OpenAI-compatible API with streaming, tool calling, vision; Agent Swarm orchestration endpoints

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness

evidence.value: OpenAI-compatible so mainstream SDKs work; first-party tooling thinner than Western providers

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance

evidence.value: K2.6 supersedes K2.5/K2; prior weights remain available, but cadence is fast

Kimi K2.7-Code released 2026-06-12: coding-specialized open-weight model built on K2.6 (1T/32B active, 256K context, Modified MIT) with ~30% lower reasoning-token usage; vendor claims +21.8% on Kimi Code Bench v2 over K2.6 — all K2.7 benchmarks are Moonshot-proprietary with no independent public-suite results yet

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of versioning practices and weight availability

evidence.value: Basic usage dashboard; self-hosted observability is deployer-built

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of available monitoring tools and metrics

evidence.value: GitHub and community support; limited English-language enterprise support

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of documentation, community, and support responsiveness

evidence.value: Available on OpenRouter and major open-model hosts; vLLM/SGLang support with native INT4

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of third-party hosting, integrations, and tooling

evidence.value: MIT with an attribution-UI requirement for deployments exceeding 100M MAU or $20M/month revenue

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions; attribution clause is trust-relevant for large-scale commercial use

operational_excellence.notes: Strong open-model ecosystem presence. Modified MIT license is permissive for most users but the attribution clause above 100M MAU / $20M monthly revenue requires legal review at hyperscale.

code-generation.notes: Vendor-reported 80.2% SWE-Bench Verified and 58.6 SWE-Bench Pro; Agent Swarm excels at long-horizon multi-file engineering. For pure coding workloads, Moonshot's coding-specialized K2.7-Code (June 2026, built on K2.6) claims further gains with ~30% lower token usage.

customer-support.notes: Capable but not specialized; agentic latency unnecessary for simple support flows.

content-creation.notes: Solid long-form generation with large context; not its differentiator.

data-analysis.notes: Strong tool-augmented analysis; Agent Swarm parallelizes multi-source investigation well.

research-assistant.notes: 54.0 HLE-with-tools and 262K context make it strong for deep, tool-driven research.

legal-compliance.notes: China-jurisdiction first-party API and absent Western certifications are blockers unless self-hosted.

healthcare.notes: Not recommended via first-party API; self-hosted deployment in a compliant environment is the only viable path.

financial-analysis.notes: Strong quantitative and agentic capability; data residency requires self-hosting for regulated firms.

education.notes: Strong STEM and coding tutoring at competitive pricing.

creative-writing.notes: Competent creative output; optimized for agentic engineering rather than prose.

strengths: Vendor-reported open-weight leadership in agentic coding (80.2% SWE-Bench Verified, 58.6 SWE-Bench Pro vs GPT-5.4's 57.7)
Agent Swarm scales to 300 sub-agents and 4,000 coordinated steps for long-horizon tasks
Open weights with near-MIT license enable full self-hosting and data control
Efficient inference: 32B active of 1T total, MLA attention, native INT4 quantization
262,144-token context with text and vision modalities
Competitive API pricing (~$0.95/$4.00 per 1M tokens) and broad availability via OpenRouter
limitations: First-party Moonshot API processes data under Chinese jurisdiction with limited Western compliance certifications
Headline benchmarks are vendor-reported and await independent replication
Modified MIT license imposes attribution-UI requirement above 100M MAU or $20M/month revenue
Self-hosting a 1T-parameter MoE requires substantial GPU infrastructure even at INT4
Limited published bias, safety, and red-team evaluations
English-language enterprise support is thin compared to Western providers
best_for: Long-horizon autonomous coding and multi-agent engineering workflows
Organizations wanting frontier-level open weights they can self-host for data control
Tool-augmented research and deep analysis at competitive cost
Cost-sensitive teams needing top-tier agentic coding via OpenRouter or first-party API
not_recommended_for: Regulated Western workloads (healthcare, legal, finance) on the first-party API
Hyperscale consumer products unwilling to satisfy the attribution-UI license clause
Teams without GPU infrastructure who also cannot accept China-jurisdiction processing

pricing.notes: First-party Moonshot API pricing confirmed July 2026; cached input drops to $0.16 per 1M (~83% off). Third-party hosts on OpenRouter vary (some cheaper, e.g. $0.55/$2.00). Self-hosting cost is infrastructure-dependent.

metadata.license: Modified MIT (attribution-UI requirement above 100M MAU or $20M/month revenue)
metadata.architecture: Mixture-of-Experts: 1T total / 32B active parameters, 384 experts, Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA), native INT4
